name: LEAPR
description:
LEAPR

The LEAPR module prepares the thermal scattering law S(α,β) in ENDF-6 format.
It generates a complete File 7 (thermal neutron scattering law) that can be used by THERMR/ACER to produce thermal scattering data for transport codes.
LEAPR is a standalone generator — it takes no input tapes and produces an output ENDF tape containing the scattering law.
The module supports continuous frequency spectra, discrete oscillators, translational modes, and optionally a secondary scatterer and pair-correlation function (coherent elastic).
tooltip: Prepare thermal scattering law S(α,β) in ENDF-6 format

parameters.name: nout
parameters.help:
'nout'

Unit for output ENDF tape containing the thermal scattering law.

parameters.name: mat
parameters.help:
'mat'

Material number for the output ENDF file. LEAPR uses non-standard MAT numbers (e.g. 1 for H in H2O, 31 for graphite).

parameters.name: za
parameters.help:
'za'

1000*Z + A for the principal scatterer (e.g. 1001 for H, 6012 for C-12).

parameters.name: awr
parameters.help:
'awr'

Atomic weight ratio of the principal scatterer (mass relative to the neutron).

parameters.name: spr
parameters.help:
'spr'

Free-atom cross section (barns) of the principal scatterer.

parameters.name: npr
parameters.help:
'npr'

Number of principal scattering atoms in the compound (e.g. 2 for H in H2O).

(default=1)

parameters.name: iel
parameters.help:
'iel'

Coherent elastic option:
  • 0 None (default for hydrogenous moderators)
  • 1 Graphite
  • 2 Beryllium / BeO
(default=0)

parameters.name: ncold
parameters.help:
'ncold'

Cold hydrogen/deuterium moderator flag:
  • 0 None
  • 1 Ortho hydrogen
  • 2 Para hydrogen
  • 3 Ortho deuterium
  • 4 Para deuterium
(default=0)

parameters.name: nsk
parameters.help:
'nsk'

Skold approximation control for pair-correlation function:
  • 0 None
  • 1 Vineyard approximation
  • 2 Skold approximation
(default=0)

parameters.name: nss
parameters.help:
'nss'

Number of secondary scatterer types:
  • 0 None
  • 1 SCT approximation
  • 2 Free gas
(default=0)

parameters.name: b7
parameters.help:
'b7'

Secondary scatterer calculation control:
  • 0 Compute S(α,β) from input spectrum (detailed)
  • 1 Use SCT/free-gas approximation only
(default=0)

parameters.name: aws
parameters.help:
'aws'

Atomic weight ratio of the secondary scatterer.

parameters.name: sps
parameters.help:
'sps'

Free-atom cross section (barns) of the secondary scatterer.

parameters.name: mss
parameters.help:
'mss'

Number of secondary scattering atoms in the compound (e.g. 1 for O in H2O).

parameters.name: lat
parameters.help:
'lat'

Alpha/beta lattice flag:
  • 0 α and β values are unscaled
  • 1 α and β values are scaled by 0.0253/kT
(default=1)

parameters.name: isabt
parameters.help:
'isabt'

S(α,β) output type:
  • 0 S(α,β) (symmetric)
  • 1 S(α,β)·exp(−β/2) (asymmetric)
(default=0)

parameters.name: ilog
parameters.help:
'ilog'

Log flag for S storage:
  • 0 Store S directly
  • 1 Store log(S)
(default=0)

parameters.name: smin
parameters.help:
'smin'

Minimum value of S(α,β) to store. Values below this are set to zero.

(default=1e-75)

parameters.name: nphon
parameters.help:
'nphon'

Maximum number of terms in the phonon expansion.

(default=100)

parameters.name: iprint
parameters.help:
'iprint'

Print flag:
  • min: Minimum output (0)
  • max: Extended output (1)
(default=min)
